src: πικρὸν καὶ ἐλεεινὸν ἀπωδύροντο ἐπὶ τῶν πρωτοτόκων καρπῶν.
lit: They were lamenting bitter and pitiable things over the offspring of the firstborn.
sm: They lamented bitterly and pitiably over their firstborn offspring.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the prepositional phrase gives the object or occasion of lament.
syn.svo: Subject: the animals of the previous lines, implied from context. Verb: ἀπωδύροντο. Accusative internal object: πικρὸν καὶ ἐλεεινόν. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῶν πρωτοτόκων καρπῶν.
syn.rhet: Closes the preceding animal-lament scene with doubled affective adjectives.

src: οἰμωγὴ δέ τις καὶ κοπετὸς ἐπὶ τῇ τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἀπωλείᾳ ἐγένετο,
lit: And a certain wailing and lamentation came to be over the destruction of the men,
sm: And a great wailing and lamentation arose over the destruction of the people,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 193 adds a second ἐπὶ-phrase by continuation.
syn.svo: Compound subject: οἰμωγὴ ... καὶ κοπετός. Verb: ἐγένετο. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῇ τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἀπωλείᾳ.
syn.rhet: The paired nouns οἰμωγή / κοπετός intensify the mournful scene.

src: ἐπὶ τῇ τῶν πρωτοτόκων νεκρῶν.
lit: Over the [destruction] of the dead firstborn.
sm: over the death of the firstborn dead.
emd: Elliptical continuation of line 192; an understood noun such as ἀπωλείᾳ is supplied only in interpretation, not in the text.

syn.svo: Prepositional phrase parallel to ἐπὶ τῇ τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἀπωλείᾳ. The governing noun is elliptical from the previous line.
syn.rhet: A compressed echo of the previous phrase tightens the focus from people generally to the firstborn specifically.

src: ὅλη γὰρ ἐπώζεσεν Αἴγυπτος ἐπὶ τῶν ἀτάφων σωμάτων.
lit: For all Egypt <UNCERTAIN: wailed?> over the unburied bodies.
sm: For all Egypt wailed over the unburied bodies.
emd: The verb ἐπώζεσεν is retained as transmitted; its exact lemma is uncertain, but the context clearly requires a lamenting sense.

syn.svo: Subject: ὅλη Αἴγυπτος. Verb: ἐπώζεσεν. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῶν ἀτάφων σωμάτων.
syn.rhet: Hyperbolic totalization: the whole land is personified in mourning.

src: ἦν δὲ θεάσασθαι φοβερὸν θέαμα
lit: And it was a fearful spectacle to behold
sm: And it was a fearful spectacle to behold,

syn.sub: The infinitive θεάσασθαι complements the impersonal ἦν.
syn.svo: Verb: ἦν. Predicate expression: φοβερὸν θέαμα. Complementary infinitive: θεάσασθαι.
syn.rhet: Transition to a visual tableau announced in elevated style.

src: τῶν Αἰγυπτίων μητέρας λυσικόμους,
lit: The Egyptian mothers with loosened hair,
sm: Egyptian mothers with hair loosed,

syn.mc: Accusative phrase dependent on the spectacle of line 195.
syn.svo: Object of the implied visual apprehension under θεάσασθαι: μητέρας λυσικόμους. Genitive τῶν Αἰγυπτίων depends on μητέρας.
syn.rhet: First tableau detail in the fearsome spectacle.

src: πατέρας λυσίφρονας,
lit: Fathers distraught,
sm: fathers beside themselves,

syn.mc: Second accusative tableau element dependent on line 195.
syn.svo: Object of the implied seeing under θεάσασθαι: πατέρας λυσίφρονας.
syn.rhet: The pair mothers/fathers universalizes the household catastrophe.

src: δεινὸν ἀνακωκύοντας τῇ αἰγυπτιακῇ φωνῇ᾽
tok: pres act ptcp acc pl m
lit: Wailing terribly with the Egyptian voice:
sm: wailing terribly in the Egyptian voice:

syn.mc: Participial accusative phrase continuing the spectacle of lines 195-197.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the raised dot introduces the quoted lament in line 199.
syn.svo: The participle ἀνακωκύοντας modifies the accusative parents of lines 196-197. Dative phrase: τῇ αἰγυπτιακῇ φωνῇ.
syn.ptc: ἀνακωκύοντας is descriptive and leads directly into the content of the cry.
syn.rhet: The lament now breaks into direct speech.

src: Δυστυχεῖς ἠτεκνώμεθα ὑπὸ μίαν ῥοπὴν ἀπὸ τοῦ πρωτοτόκου καρποῦ.
lit: Wretched [are] we; we were made childless within one moment of the firstborn offspring.
sm: Wretched are we: in a single instant we were bereft of our firstborn offspring.

syn.svo: Subject: implied ἡμεῖς. Verb: ἠτεκνώμεθα. Predicative adjective: Δυστυχεῖς. Temporal phrase: ὑπὸ μίαν ῥοπήν. Ablative phrase: ἀπὸ τοῦ πρωτοτόκου καρποῦ.
syn.rhet: The direct cry condenses the catastrophe into a single instant.

src: ἦσαν δὲ ἐπὶ μασθῶν κοπτόμενοι.,
tok: pres mp ptcp nom pl m
lit: And they were beating themselves upon their breasts,
sm: And they were beating their breasts in lament,

syn.svo: Subject: the mourners, implied from context. Verb: ἦσαν. Participial complement: κοπτόμενοι. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ μασθῶν.
syn.ptc: κοπτόμενοι is complementary/descriptive with ἦσαν, depicting continuous mourning action.
syn.rhet: Returns from direct speech to gestural lament.

src: χερσὶν τύπτοντες κροτήματα ἐπὶ τῆς τῶν νεκρῶν ὀρχήσεως.
tok: pres act ptcp nom pl m
lit: Striking beats with hands upon the dance of the dead.
sm: striking rhythms with their hands over the dance of the dead.

syn.svo: Implied subject: the mourners. Participle: τύπτοντες. Object: κροτήματα. Instrumental dative: χερσίν. Prepositional phrase: ἐπὶ τῆς τῶν νεκρῶν ὀρχήσεως.
syn.ptc: τύπτοντες elaborates the manner of the mourning scene.
syn.rhet: The phrase turns lament into almost choreographed funeral movement.

src: Τοιαύτη συμφορὰ περιέσχεν Αἴγυπτον,
lit: Such a disaster encompassed Egypt,
sm: Such a disaster overtook Egypt,

syn.mc: Main clause summarizing the preceding lament scene.
syn.svo: Subject: Τοιαύτη συμφορά. Verb: περιέσχεν. Object: Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: A resumptive summary rounds off the tableau.

src: ἄφνω δὲ ἠτέκνωσεν αὐτήν.
lit: And suddenly it made her childless.
sm: and suddenly it made her childless.

syn.svo: Subject: implied συμφορά. Verb: ἠτέκνωσεν. Object: αὐτήν (= Αἴγυπτον). Adverb: ἄφνω.
syn.rhet: A sudden adverb sharpens the violence of the blow.

src: ἦν δὲ ᾿Ισραὴλ φρουρούμενος ὑπὸ τῆς τοῦ προβάτου σφαγῆς,
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: But Israel was being guarded by the slaughter of the sheep,
sm: But Israel was being guarded by the slaughter of the sheep,

syn.mc: Main clause contrasting Egypt with Israel.
syn.svo: Subject: ᾿Ισραὴλ. Verb: ἦν with participial complement φρουρούμενος. Agent/instrument phrase: ὑπὸ τῆς τοῦ προβάτου σφαγῆς.
syn.ptc: φρουρούμενος is complementary with ἦν, expressing Israel's protected state.
syn.rhet: Sharp antithesis: Egypt bereaved, Israel guarded.

src: καί γε συνεφωτίζετο ὑπὸ τοῦ χυθέντος αἵματος,
tok: aor pass ptcp gen sg n
lit: And indeed he was illumined by the blood that had been poured out,
sm: and indeed he was illumined by the blood that had been poured out,

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the participle χυθέντος modifies αἵματος.
syn.svo: Subject: Israel, understood from line 206. Verb: συνεφωτίζετο. Agent/instrument phrase: ὑπὸ τοῦ χυθέντος αἵματος.
syn.rhet: Protection is now expressed as illumination as well as guarding.

src: καὶ τεῖχος ηὑρίσκετο τοῦ λαοῦ ὃ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος.
ana: καὶ τεῖχος ηὑρίσκετο τοῦ λαοῦ ὁ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος.
lit: And as a wall of the people was found the death of the sheep.
sm: And the death of the sheep was found to be a wall for the people.
emd: The raw ὃ before τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος is analyzed as ὁ, since the article is syntactically required here although this correction is not separately listed in change_log.txt.

syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος. Verb: ηὑρίσκετο. Predicate nominative: τεῖχος. Dependent genitive: τοῦ λαοῦ.
syn.rhet: The sacrificial death becomes defensive architecture in vivid metaphor.

src: ὦ μυστηρίου καινοῦ καὶ ἀνεκδιηγήτου
lit: O of a new and indescribable mystery
sm: O the wonder of a new and indescribable mystery!
emd: Exclamatory genitive with an omitted head noun such as θαῦμα is understood in interpretation only.

syn.svo: No finite verb. Genitive phrase μυστηρίου καινοῦ καὶ ἀνεκδιηγήτου depends on an implied exclamatory head noun.
syn.rhet: A rhetorical outcry interrupts narration with wonder.

src: ἡ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγὴ ηὑὐρίσκετο τοῦ ᾿Ισραὴλ σωτηρία,
ana: ἡ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγὴ ηὑρίσκετο τοῦ ᾿Ισραὴλ σωτηρία,
lit: The slaughter of the sheep was found [to be] salvation of Israel,
sm: The slaughter of the sheep was found to be Israel's salvation,

syn.mc: Main clause continuing the exclamatory interpretation.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγή. Verb: ηὑρίσκετο. Predicate nominative: σωτηρία. Dependent genitive: τοῦ ᾿Ισραήλ.
syn.rhet: The sacrificial act is reinterpreted soteriologically.

src: καὶ ὃ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος ζωὴ τοῦ λαοῦ ἐγένετο,
ana: καὶ ὁ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος ζωὴ τοῦ λαοῦ ἐγένετο,
lit: And the death of the sheep became life of the people,
sm: And the death of the sheep became life for the people,
emd: The raw ὃ is analyzed as ὁ, since an article is required syntactically here though this correction is not separately listed in change_log.txt.

syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος. Verb: ἐγένετο. Predicate nominative: ζωή. Dependent genitive: τοῦ λαοῦ.
syn.rhet: Paradoxical inversion: death becomes life.

src: καὶ τὸ αἷμα ἐδυσώπησεν τὸν ἄγγελον.
lit: And the blood appeased the angel.
sm: And the blood appeased the angel.

syn.svo: Subject: τὸ αἷμα. Verb: ἐδυσώπησεν. Object: τὸν ἄγγελον.
syn.rhet: Third succinct clause completes the explanatory triad: salvation, life, appeasement.

src: λέγε μοι, ὦ ἄγγελε, τί ἐδυσωπήθης;
lit: Tell me, O angel, by what were you appeased?
sm: Tell me, O angel, what was it that appeased you?

syn.sub: The question is elaborated by the alternative accusatives of lines 214-216.
syn.svo: Verb: λέγε. Indirect object: μοι. Vocative: ὦ ἄγγελε. Interrogative verb: ἐδυσωπήθης with τί as the questioned object/cause.
syn.rhet: Apostrophe to the angel dramatizes the interpretive turn.

src: τὴν τοῦ προβάτου σφαγὴν ἢ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου ζωήν;
lit: The slaughter of the sheep, or the life of the Lord?
sm: Was it the slaughter of the sheep, or the life of the Lord?

syn.mc: Alternative accusative phrase dependent on the question of line 213.
syn.svo: Elliptical alternatives to the questioned cause of ἐδυσωπήθης: τὴν ... σφαγήν / τὴν ... ζωήν.
syn.rhet: The first antithetical pair opposes type and fulfillment.

src: τὸν τοῦ προβάτου θάνατον ἢ τὸν τοῦ κυρίου τύπον;
lit: The death of the sheep, or the type of the Lord?
sm: Was it the death of the sheep, or the Lord's type?

syn.mc: Second alternative pair dependent on line 213.
syn.svo: Elliptical accusative alternatives: τὸν ... θάνατον / τὸν ... τύπον.
syn.rhet: The pair presses the typological question more sharply.

src: τὸ τοῦ προβάτου αἷμα ἣ τὸ τοῦ κυρίου πνεῦμα;
ana: τὸ τοῦ προβάτου αἷμα ἢ τὸ τοῦ κυρίου πνεῦμα;
lit: The blood of the sheep, or the spirit of the Lord?
sm: Was it the blood of the sheep, or the spirit of the Lord?

syn.mc: Third alternative pair dependent on the question of line 213.
syn.svo: Elliptical accusative alternatives: τὸ ... αἷμα / τὸ ... πνεῦμα.
syn.rhet: The triadic questioning culminates in blood versus spirit.

src: δῆλος εἶ δυσωπηθεὶς
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg m
lit: You are clear, having been appeased,
sm: It is clear that you were appeased,

syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle explains the evident state.
syn.svo: Subject: implied σύ. Verb: εἶ. Predicate adjective: δῆλος. Participle: δυσωπηθείς.
syn.ptc: δυσωπηθείς is circumstantial-causal, explaining why the fact is clear.
syn.rhet: The question now turns into confident inference.

src: ἰδὼν τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον ἐν τῷ προβάτῳ γινόμενον,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
pres mid ptcp acc sg n
lit: Having seen the mystery of the Lord coming to pass in the sheep,
sm: because you saw the mystery of the Lord taking place in the sheep,

syn.sub: No finite subordinate clause; the participle provides causal explanation.
syn.svo: Implied subject: the angel. Participle: ἰδών. Object: τὸ τοῦ κυρίου μυστήριον. Secondary participle: γινόμενον modifying μυστήριον. Prepositional phrase: ἐν τῷ προβάτῳ.
syn.ptc: ἰδών is causal; γινόμενον is attributive to μυστήριον.
syn.rhet: The interpretive key is explicit: the Lord's mystery was seen within the type.

src: τὴν τοῦ κυρίου ζωὴν ἐν τῇ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγῇ,
lit: The life of the Lord in the slaughter of the sheep,
sm: the life of the Lord in the slaughter of the sheep,

syn.mc: Accusative phrase in apposition to the mystery seen in line 218.
syn.svo: Accusative phrase dependent on ἰδών / seeing: τὴν τοῦ κυρίου ζωήν. Prepositional phrase: ἐν τῇ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγῇ.
syn.rhet: First appositional unpacking of the mystery: life in slaughter.

src: τὸν τοῦ κυρίου τύπον ἐν τῷ τοῦ προβάτου θανάτῳ,
lit: The type of the Lord in the death of the sheep,
sm: the Lord's type in the death of the sheep,

syn.mc: Second accusative appositional phrase continuing line 218.
syn.svo: Accusative phrase dependent on ἰδών: τὸν τοῦ κυρίου τύπον. Prepositional phrase: ἐν τῷ τοῦ προβάτου θανάτῳ.
syn.rhet: Second unpacking of the mystery: the Lord's type disclosed in the sheep's death.
